sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Daedalus Wallet maintenance and improvements for 2026-2027, including protocol maintenance, ecosystem expansion, user support, hardware wallet support, CIP-30, Japanese localisation, and architecture assessment.
Recipient: Se7en Labs, Inc., with Intersect serving as administrator and milestone verifier

Deliverables: Compatible Daedalus releases at least 2 weeks before each mainnet hard fork, Continuous cardano-node and cardano-wallet integration and platform maintenance for Windows, macOS x86_64, macOS aarch64, and Linux, Keystone and Flex hardware wallet support in a stable release, CIP-30 dApp connector implementation in Daedalus with documentation, Ongoing English and Japanese user support and full Japanese translation for shipped features, Public architecture assessment with recommendation and resource estimates
Deadline/expiry: 52-week contract period, with architecture assessment published by Q3 2027

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Daedalus is Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet and derives wallet and governance data directly from the chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Daedalus has approximately 4,000 monthly active users according to opt-in telemetry, while the proposer says the true count is meaningfully higher.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal funds a time-and-materials engagement covering protocol maintenance, Keystone and Flex hardware wallet support, CIP-30 dApp connector work, Japanese localisation, and user support.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): All funded outputs are described as public assets under the Apache License 2.0, including source code, build tooling, documentation, and release artifacts.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Se7en Labs says it assumed Daedalus responsibility under an IOG contract in January 2026 and shipped Mithril Snapshot Bootstrap, UTxO-HD/LSM backend integration, native Apple Silicon builds, a release CLI, and Nix build modernization.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal says release-timing and version-currency success metrics are independently verifiable from public GitHub repositories and the Cardano mainnet chain.
missing: Independent evidence for: Daedalus is Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet and derives wallet and governance data directly from the chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends.
Independent evidence for: Daedalus has approximately 4,000 monthly active users according to opt-in telemetry, while the proposer says the true count is meaningfully higher.

Independent evidence for: Se7en Labs says it assumed Daedalus responsibility under an IOG contract in January 2026 and shipped Mithril Snapshot Bootstrap, UTxO-HD/LSM backend integration, native Apple Silicon builds, a release CLI, and Nix build modernization.
Independent evidence for: The proposer states that any unspent labor, hardware, or audit budget will be returned to the treasury.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal funds a time-and-materials engagement covering protocol maintenance, Keystone and Flex hardware wallet support, CIP-30 dApp connector work, Japanese localisation, and user support." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Daedalus is Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet and derives wallet and governance data directly from the chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
